key_insight: SevenNet-Polar extends an equivariant GNN to predict Born effective charges alongside energy, forces, and stress, delivering accurate charge-aware materials simulations at scale.
engineering_relevance: The model makes electric-field and polarization-sensitive atomistic simulation much faster and more practical for complex oxides, defects, and large supercell systems.
method: Built on the SevenNet equivariant graph neural network architecture.
Trained multitask models for energy, forces, stress, and Born effective charges.
Used FlashTP acceleration to support large-scale multi-GPU and single-GPU inference.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports BEC-only RMSE as low as 0.0043 e and multitask accuracy of 1.0 meV/atom for energy, 12 meV/angstrom for forces, 0.05 GPa for stress, and 0.0029 e for BEC.
watch_item: BEC scaling is less favorable than energy/force/stress, and generalization to unseen environments should still be validated for each new material class.
paper_title: SevenNet-Polar for MultiTask Prediction of Energy, Forces, Stress, and Born Effective Charges: Development and Application to ZrO$_2$, Li$_3$PO$_4$, and Perovskites
